title: cancrinia submarginata
intro: Cancrinia submarginata, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy or prickly poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cancrinia submarginata include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its use as an antispasmodic and antitussive, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues and muscle spasms. Cancrinia submarginata is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or ointment, with its herbal preparations typically involving the extraction of its alkaloid-rich roots and leaves.
